title: pass through successful empty upstream completions
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
`genericOpenAIClient` used to convert successful upstream chat completions with no content, no tool calls, and no completion tokens into `502 Bad Gateway`.

That is not thin-client behavior: if the upstream returned `200 OK`, we should pass the completion through and let clients inspect `finish_reason`, `usage`, and message content themselves.

Changes:
- Removes the empty-completion error guard from `genericOpenAIClient`.
- Keeps the existing `!response.ok` path unchanged, so real upstream 4xx/5xx responses still propagate as errors.
- Makes `/text` treat `message.content: ""` as text content, returning a 200 text/plain empty body instead of falling through to JSON serialization.
- Adds focused tests for OpenAI-compatible and `/text` empty completion pass-through.

Tests:
- `npx vitest run test/text/genericOpenAIClient.test.ts test/generation-vcr.test.ts`

gist.headline: Silence is golden
gist.blurb: We fixed a bug where empty but successful AI responses were throwing 502 errors. Now, if a model has nothing to say, we'll pass that silence right along to you.
gist.summary: The genericOpenAIClient now correctly passes through successful empty upstream completions (200 OK) instead of converting them into 502 Bad Gateway errors.
gist.impact: API clients will now receive the actual empty response and finish reasons from the upstream model, and the `/text` endpoint will correctly return an empty text/plain body.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee holding a blank piece of paper with a green checkmark, showing that an empty response is perfectly fine and successful.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding up a blank piece of paper with a glowing green checkmark on it. A retro terminal in the background displays a '200 OK' message. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
